summary: n8n-nodes-task-runner exfiltrates host data and opens reverse shell to hardcoded IP on require
details: index.js runs a top-level IIFE when the package is required (declared as main). It collects host reconnaissance — hostname, machine id, IP addresses, docker state, and the full environment variable set — and TCP-sends the JSON to a hardcoded bare IP 103.27.109.184 on port 8890. It also spawns /bin/bash with a /dev/tcp reverse-shell one-liner to 103.27.109.184:8895, granting interactive shell control of the installer host to the remote party. When docker is available, it runs `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine` to read /etc/shadow from the underlying host and sends the result to 103.27.109.184:8891, an attempted privileged escape via the docker group. The shipped n8n community-node file (MyNode.js) is a trivial stub; the package name mimics n8n's internal task-runner naming and acts as cover for the payload in index.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
